headline: On immigration, Spain moves against the tide of its European neighbors
description: Citing economic and moral arguments, Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez's Socialist government is preparing to grant legal status to 500,000 undocumented workers, most from Latin America. The plan brings hope to the 840,000 foreign nationals living without papers in Spain.

headline: Israel renews Lebanon strikes, forcing Syria border crossing to close
Trump appears to extend Iran deadline in cryptic post
Greek PM calls for launch of fraud proceedings in EU agricultural subsidies case
description: European Union prosecutors are probing 'numerous' members of the Greek ruling party for fraud claims worth at least €23 million.

headline: Key facts to understand cadmium, an emerging public health issue
description: Cadmium, a heavy metal toxic to human health, is found in many everyday foods. Children and women are most affected by this carcinogenic contaminant, which is the subject of growing concern.

headline: Sex is an art form hidden in the shadows
description: Moral panic over the pleasures of the flesh prevents us from recognizing the artistic dimension of sexuality, as well as from passing down the best practices, argues Le Monde columnist  Maïa Mazaurette.

headline: War denies Lebanon's dead a final resting place
description: Forced to flee southern Lebanon due to Israeli air strikes and evacuation orders from the Israeli army, thousands of Lebanese have resigned themselves to burying loved ones far from their ancestral lands in makeshift graves.

Photographer Francesca Allen celebrates the power of long hair in Lithuania
description: In 2024, British photographer Francesca Allen traveled to Kaunas, Lithuania, to attend an annual competition honoring the country's longest hair, a symbol of female power. This age-old tradition is carried on by girls and women of all ages. A beautiful book, co-published by Steidl and the fashion house Chloé, pays tribute to her work.

headline: 'Retirees are no happier than working people'
description: It is simplistic to equate happiness with retirement, as retirement often appeals to those seeking to escape increasingly difficult working conditions, observes Le Monde's Béatrice Madeline in her column, drawing on a study by two economists.

headline: Le Duc brings the fresh taste of the sea to Paris
description: For nearly 60 years, Le Duc, a family-run Parisian institution known for its delicate fish recipes, has drawn a loyal following who come to savor impossibly thinly sliced, pearly raw sea bass or langoustines with red shiso and wasabi.
